Just about everything. In desperate need of TLC. One of the worse hotels I've stayed in. 
I had a bad feeling as soon as the taxi pulled up. 
Reception smelt of cigarettes. 
I stayed on the 6th floor and upon exiting the lift there was no lights. Had to use the light on my phone to find my way. 
Door to room was not a swipe type where you place your card onto the ready. You need to open it with a physical key?!

reviews.text:
Đây là khạch sạn tệ nhất và kinh khủng nhất mà tôi từng trải nghiệm:
1. Khách sạn rất cũ, đồ đạc, giường, tủ... như đồ phế liệu
2. Mùi hôi thật kinh khủng
3. Phòng kiểu như rò rỉ điện, khi tôi yêu cầu đổi vì không an toàn cháy nổ, nhân viên bảo hết phòng. Tôi làm căng đòi báo cáo và hoàn tiền thì mới đổi qua phòng mới nhưng cũng không khá hơn.
Khách sạn này là 1 kiểu lừa đảo, hình ảnh trên web khác hoàn toàn (tận 90%-95% thực tế)
Thật sự đây là khách sạn dơ, quá tệ và không an toàn về cháy nổ
Tôi khuyên bạn nên bỏ qua khách sạn này!

reviews.title: Nightmare hotel
reviews.positives: The location is good.
reviews.negatives:
The bed is too hard and too low. If you have knee or back  problem, this is not a good choice. 
The room is very small that there is not enough space for you to open your suitcase. We have only carry on luggage but still have no space to open it.
When we came to the room, the chairs were wet. They just washed it few hours ago and did not dry it off.
They sprayed something in the bathroom and the room that you can hardly breath. We found that they hang a small bag at the air conditioner. After we asked them to remove it, the smell in the room is less, but yet not a pleasant smell at all.
Next to the hotel reception is the area for massage. In order to go to the elevator, you have to pass thu this area and see the customers receiving massage. 
The elevator is very small and not clean.
The bed is next to the window that if you open the curtain, the people from the other house will see you immediately. 
We have two beds and there is not enough space between beds even thought one bed is against the window and the other is next to the bathroom door..
The room is not like the one showing on Booking.com.

reviews.title: A very serious health hazard,  mold on a level you cant imagine
reviews.text: I'm a tough traveler.  I never leave bad reviews.  I usually am just fine in hotels where they have a couple bad reviews.  The place is a serious medical hazard.  The rooms have no windows, The easily replaceable wall fans in the bathroom they refuse to replace.  It was the most disgusting thing I've ever seen in my life.  They could literally fix it in one day by buying a new fan and bleaching the whole room, but they're pure greed and arrogance is unfathomable. They will risk your health for a tiny cheap profit.  I will never use agoda again,   any review from a  Vietnamese name is obviously super fake.  RUN!!!!

reviews.title: Dingy and Run Down
reviews.text:
The description says that it was newly renovated in 2024. If what they mean by newly renovated is slapping a thing coat of new paint on furniture and walls, then yes, it was newly renovated. This is by far the worst hotel room that I have stayed at in SE Asia and I travel a lot! I have been to Cambodia, Vietnam, Malaysia, Thailand, South Korea, Tawain...this hotel was by far the worst I have ever been in. 

The bathtub was disgusting. The floor boards and baseboards were rotten and run down. The AC was old and emitted a musky smell. They had an oil diffuser in my room to help with the smell. 

There was mold/mildew growing above the AC and the elevator smelled like trash. At one point they had trash piled in the stairwell, so if you wanted to take the stairs, you had to literally leap over a pile of garbage. 

I have no idea how this is rated as highly as it is. Stay away!

reviews.text:
I had an extremely bad experience booking this hotel. The actual room was completely different from the photos shown on Agoda. When I checked in, the room had a bad smell, was damp, moldy, dirty, and felt very uncomfortable. It was impossible to rest properly there.

What was even more disappointing was that when I arrived, the hotel assigned me a room that was not the type of room I had booked. The room was located on the 8th floor — the highest floor — but the elevator only went up to the 7th floor, meaning guests had to walk up to the 8th floor with their luggage. This was not clearly disclosed before booking.

I had booked this hotel for 6 nights, but I simply could not stay in a room in that condition. Although I reported the issue to the hotel reception and contacted Agoda for support, neither party provided a proper solution and both avoided responsibility. With no other option, I was forced to give up the money I had paid for those 6 nights and spend additional money to find another hotel.

This experience left me extremely disappointed. I lost both money and time, and it seriously affected my trip. I would not recommend this hotel to anyone who expects a clean place to stay, accurate photos and descriptions, and responsible customer service.

reviews.positives: La verdad no me gusto nada este alojamiento. Esta considerado un 3 estrellas pero en teoria no califica ni como media estrella.
reviews.negatives:
El personal de recepción no contaba con el nivel mínimo indispensable de inglés y, a diferencia de otros alojamientos en los que nos hospedamos en Vietnam, no mostró el más mínimo interés en comunicarse con los clientes. Nada más llegar, preguntamos a la recepcionista si podía ayudarnos a ubicar el hotel en un mapa impreso que llevábamos, y no supo hacerlo.

La habitación asignada se encontraba en mal estado y era muy reducida. Las camas eran duras, las sabanas y fundas viejas, la cortina de la ducha era muy antigua y de muy baja calidad, el frigorífico parecía haber sido recogido de un vertedero, y el techo del baño presentaba filtraciones y manchas visibles. El lavamanos del baño estaba cuarteado. Además, el espacio disponible en la habitación era claramente insuficiente para moverse con comodidad. Al final solo dormimos una noche ahi y al dia siguiente temprano en la mañana nos cambiamos a un hotel en mejores condiciones en la misma calle.

Rogamos consulten las fotografías adjuntas, que ilustran claramente las deficiencias descritas.

reviews.title: Old, dirty, and smelly room
reviews.text:
I booked this room because it was advertised as a newly renovated hotel, located in the city center, and offering a big discount. However, the reality was completely different from the photos.

The room was old, had a damp smell, and the furniture was covered in dust. The mattress was thin and hard; the bed sheet and blanket were old, torn, and even stained yellow in some areas. The towels were also reused ones — although they had a freshly washed scent, they were thin and worn out from long-term use.

The soundproofing was terrible. I could clearly hear the neighboring room opening and closing their door and talking.

Although the hotel is conveniently located in the city center, I do not recommend staying here — whether you're traveling for leisure or business — because you simply won't get a good night's sleep.

reviews.title: No Budget Saved, No Cleanliness, Low Facility
reviews.text:
First of all, the location is great. There are many local food options around the area.
However, the problem is the hotel itself. I don't even want to mention the facilities because they are extremely poor. What is more concerning is the lack of cleanliness and hygiene.

When I arrived at the hotel and entered the room, I immediately noticed a strong smell, like body odor or the smell of old shoes, which seemed to come from the spa on the ground floor. At first, I thought it was just on that floor, but after checking in and taking the elevator (which is very, very small and smelled terrible), I realized the problem was everywhere.

It seems like they do not clean regularly. I couldn't even see myself clearly in the mirror because it was so dirty. I stayed on the 5th floor, and the hallway was very narrow with another unpleasant smell.

As for the room, the odor was terrible. I honestly believe they did not change the bed sheets from the previous guest. I couldn’t sleep the whole night. I even thought about moving to another hotel, but since I was on a business trip, I had to stay. I stayed for five nights, and they never changed the bed linens once.

Honestly, there are many hotels around the area that are much better for the same price. I do not recommend this hotel, especially if you care about hygiene.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: We stayed at this hotel on March 21st, and the door to our room was quite difficult to open. The next night, we had another trouble opening the door to our room. It appears that the door to Room 401 is broken, making it difficult to open. We spoke to the night shift because we couldn't open the door. But when we went to seek assistance, he appeared dissatisfied. Following that day, we encountered another problem with the door. We attempted to open the door, but it was unable to open until the key broke in the lock. So we asked him to come and look. He appeared upset and told us that he had the last key, so we should be careful. He didn't even pay attention to what we said; the key had split in the door. We had to explain again how it couldn't be unlocked. When he walked up to see what had happened, he approached us and asked, "What did you do?" He made an upset expression again. We thought his service was quite terrible. From the first moment we met until the day we departed, he never smiled or frowned. He didn't seem willing to assist us at all. However, the other staff members were fine. It was not too horrible. However, we felt quite awful about what we experienced.

reviews.title: Would not recommend
reviews.text:
Our triple executive room was much smaller than expected, but because we arrived at midnight 3am and were too tired plus the receptionist was sleeping. The room was absolutely terrible: old, dark, wallpaper/paint peeling off, ants, stains on beddings, mouldy bathroom with loose taps, stained shower curtains and tub, water would flow right through the curtains onto the floor. Everything was just old and dirty. However we managed to sleep few hours. 

In morning, we spoke to the receptionist about our booking being for a larger room surely, and without any disagreements, they upgraded us to a family room. However the condition of the room was the same: stains, mould, marks everywhere. Old furniture, extremely mouldy bathroom, stained beddings, ants, dust, broken wardrobe, list continues. When we requested bedding change due to stains, they did change but the new ones had it too. 

The location was fantastic. Walkable to most attractions, bustop, restaurants, shopping etc. The receptionists are nice. 

But the hotel is just not it. It is definitely not renovated, as Agoda page says. Only the reception area is like in images, but the rooms otherwise are terrible. 

We managed as we were out most of the day but never again!

基本的なアメニティーは揃っているし、このエリアで一泊だけならありと思います。
reviews.negatives:
ホテルは、とても古く、壁がとても薄く下のフロア、上のフロアからの音が聞こえます。
綺麗な写真では、古い写真を使用しているんじゃないかと思います。

◆予約変更について
✖︎事前に、booking.comから2泊から1泊に変更できないか聞いても、全く正確な返答がないまま、まともに意思疎通もできず、聞いた内容に対して、答えも返ってきません。結局予約は、変更できませんでした。

・クレジットカード支払いがちゃんとできるのか聞いても返答なし

◆🔑部屋のドア・キー🔑について
部屋はとても古くて、ドアの鍵もちゃんと開ける事ができませんでした。スタッフに聞いても「強く引っ張ったら、勝手に開きます」と言われました。こんな適当な説明で、開けることできる人いないですよね。

自分で開けようとしてもちゃんと開けられなかったので、再度聞く必要がありました。鍵をさしたまま、鍵を左に回して、ドアを引っ張ると開く不思議な扉です🔑
・ドア用カードキーは、全く機能してなくて残念でした。

◆カードの支払いの時間帯について

このホテルの一階のマッサージを受けましたが、カードで代金を支払いたいというと、”it it too late”と言われ、すごく嫌そうな顔をされます。

カードの機械、すぐ隣のお店から、持ってきていました。
カードの機械を使い回ししてるんしゃないかと思います。

・マッサージは、おすすめしないです。
チップを求められますが、Sorry, No tips と言えばいいです。

◆各リュック、スーツケースがどれか把握していない
・どの部屋のお客様のスーツケース、リュックなのか把握していない。
→番号のタグなどをつけて、お客さんにも番号を渡して普通は、管理すると思うのですが、全くそういうこともありませんでした。

階段にタオル、水などの様々なものが、乱雑に置いてあって、清潔とは言えないです。

reviews.title: Good Location, Poor Maintenance
reviews.text:
My stay at Vintage Saigon Hotel and Spa had a mix of positives and serious concerns.

On the good side, the room was very spacious. It comfortably fit two queen-size beds and one regular bed, which worked well for our group. The view from the windows was also beautiful and added to the overall experience. The location is convenient, with a coffee shop and convenience stores nearby, making it easy to grab essentials. The staff were generally kind and welcoming, always greeting us with a smile.

However, there were several issues that really affected our stay. The bathroom lighting was quite poor, making it feel dark and inconvenient to use. The room also lacked proper mugs for hot drinks—only regular glasses without handles were provided, so we had to use a towel just to hold hot water safely.

Cleanliness was a major concern. The room was not thoroughly cleaned—there was visible dust in the corners, under the coffee table, and beneath the beds. We even found a dead cockroach and leftover trash from previous guests, which was very disappointing.

Another issue was the environment outside the hotel. Some staff were smoking near the entrance, and we had to cover our baby’s nose just to pass by, which was uncomfortable and concerning.

The biggest problem, however, was the presence of mosquitoes inside the room. My baby ended up with multiple bite marks and had trouble sleeping due to the itching. We requested assistance from housekeeping, but even after they attended to it, there were still mosquitoes flying around when we returned.

Overall, while the space, view, and staff friendliness were appreciated, the issues with cleanliness, hygiene, and pest control cannot be overlooked. I would not recommend staying here, especially for families with babies or anyone with sensitive skin.

reviews.title: Quick Stop, Simple Stay
reviews.text:
I stayed at Vintage Saigon Hotel & Spa for a short layover, and it worked fine for a quick stop in the city. The location is excellent, right in the center of Saigon, which made it easy to get around and see a few sights during my short stay.

The hotel looks nice in pictures, but in reality, it felt a bit darker than expected, and the bathroom exhaust was somewhat noisy. The room décor was simple and functional, though not particularly stylish.

Overall, it’s a budget-friendly and convenient option if you just need a place for a day or two. It’s not luxurious, but for a short stay, it’s perfectly fine—you get what you pay for.

reviews.title: Worst hotel I've stayed in
reviews.text:
This is quite possibly the worst hotel I have stayed in. The stench of mould from the visible damp patches and plaster hanging off the was repulsive. The door on the built-in wardrobe was hanging off and unable to open safely. The stench inside the wardrobe or so bad that you would not hang any clothes in. The bed sheets smelt of damp. The pillows were very old and lumpy and also stank. 
The bathroom had constant cockroaches coming out of the drains. 
The hotel advertisers as having breakfast available. There is no such facility. Even if there was I was not eat in such a dirty place. 
I booked a 40 minute foot massage on the last day. I waited 25 minutes for the girl to turn up. When she finally turned up she spent half the time playing on her telephone with one hand whilst gently rubbing oil into my feet with the other hand. This was not a massage. I could have washed my own feet. 
This hotel needs ripping out and rebuilding from the inside. I wouldn't stay there again if it was free. 
The staff were very apologetic and kept on saying that they would report everything to the owner. However it is quite obvious that the owner does not care. 
Avoid this hotel at all costs or even at no cost. 
I wouldn't even give this hotel one star. In most countries it would have been closed down by the health authorises.

reviews.title: For long-term stays
reviews.text:
This time, I stayed in a room without a window for one night. The hotel is more suited for same-sex friends who get along well or for solo travelers. The staff's response was very pleasant. They greeted and saw me off with a smile. Below, I will write my impressions as my objective feedback.

Good points:
(1) The location is very good. Within a few minutes, there are several convenience stores open 24 hours, and you can access Ben Thanh and COOP Mart on foot. Although I didn’t use it, the bus stop for the 109 bus to the airport is also close by.
(2) The bed linens and towels are clean, and I think it's sufficient for a good rest. Unusually, there is also a bathtub.
(3) Competitive pricing. In Ho Chi Minh, with the center focusing on sightseeing and dining, if you can prioritize the hotel merely for sleeping and resting, this is one of the best hotels.

Points to note:
(1) The ceiling is not high, and naturally, without a window, there is no view.
(2) In areas that don’t get sunlight, there is a slight smell of mold.
(3) The TV powers on, but I couldn’t change the channels on the STB. Since I don’t watch TV, it didn't particularly affect me.

reviews.title: Don't recommend this place
reviews.negatives:
During our stay in Vietnam, we slept in many places, and none of them were as dirty as this one. I chose this hotel because of its three stars, as these were our last days in Vietnam. This place was not one of the cheaper options, but after looking at the photos, we thought it was worth it. It turned out to be a waste of money. I’m not sure when the room was last cleaned—probably only the bedding was changed.

Moreover, the amenities listed include a coffee maker, which was not actually there. When we ran out of toilet paper and asked for some at the reception, we only received it the next day after asking again. Don’t expect any room cleaning during your stay. At the end of our stay, we also received a message from the hotel stating that they were still dealing with issues related to the COVID-19 pandemic and saying: "If our services did not deserve a 10/10 rating, we kindly ask for your understanding and request that you refrain from leaving a review." I found this very unprofessional—it seems they receive many complaints and send this message to every guest.

I’m not nitpicking about the condition and state of the room itself, as I understand they may not have the funds for renovations. However, this is about the lack of basic services, such as cleaning or even providing toilet paper, which does not meet the standard of a three-star hotel or the price we paid. I regret not staying in a hostel or homestay, where prices were much lower and cleanliness was excellent.
